evidence_quotes.quote: every game released should have an end of life plan, that patches the game on the day the server gets shutdown,

paragraphs.text: I really like to play computer games. From my youth on, I played them and from time to time I like to play really old games, which I own, with an emulator. This is possible, because those old games do not need a connection to the publishers server, just to verify, that I am allowed to play them. I mostly try to avoid those verifiying games, because the publisher could shut down the verifiying server at any time or goes bankrupt. A digital fairness, in my opinion, would be, that every game released should have an end of life plan, that patches the game on the day the server gets shutdown, so that I could play the games I own at any given point in time later in an emulator. Or that the source code is published. There are for sure lots of more possiblities, that prevent the dark pattern, of preventing to "destroy" a product, that I bought at any time.
